title: FINRA Case 25-00179 — Vinayak Birla v. Robinhood Securities, LLC
text:
Award
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
In the Matter of the Arbitration Between:
Claimant Case Number: 25-00179
Vinayak Birla
vs.
Respondent Hearing Site: Tampa, Florida
Robinhood Securities, LLC
Awards are rendered by independent arbitrators who are chosen by the parties to issue final,
binding decisions. FINRA makes available an arbitration forum—pursuant to rules approved by
the SEC—but has no part in deciding the award.
Nature of the Dispute: Customer vs. Member
This case was decided by an all-public panel.
REPRESENTATION OF PARTIES
For Claimant Vinayak Birla (“Claimant”): David M. Marco, Esq., SmithMarco, P.C., Sarasota,
Florida.
For Respondent Robinhood Securities, LLC (“Respondent”): Gerry A. Giurato, Esq., Murphy &
Anderson, P.A., Jacksonville, Florida.
CASE INFORMATION
Statement of Claim filed on or about: January 27, 2025.
Claimant signed the Submission Agreement: January 27, 2025.
Statement of Answer filed on or about: April 14, 2025.
Respondent signed the Submission Agreement: April 11, 2025.
CASE SUMMARY
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant asserted the following cause of action: violation of the
Electronic Fund Transfers Act, 15 U.S.C. §1693, et seq. The cause of action related to an
alleged $152,750.00 debited by an unknown third party on January 16, 2024, January 17, 2024,
January 18, 2024, and January 22, 2024, from Claimant’s account with Respondent, which
Claimant disputed with Respondent on February 2, 2024.
Unless specifically admitted in the Statement of Answer, Respondent denied the allegations made
in the Statement of Claim and asserted various affirmative defenses.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-00179
Award Page 2 of 4
RELIEF REQUESTED
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant requested recovery of amounts constituting unauthorized
transactions totaling $152,750.00; statutory damages allowable under 15 U.S.C.
§1693m(a)(2)(A); treble damages allowable under 15 U.S.C. §1693f(e); attorneys’ fees and
costs; and any other relief deemed appropriate by the Panel.
In the Statement of Answer, Respondent requested an award denying Claimant’s claim and
granting Respondent its attorneys’ fees and any other relief that the Panel deemed appropriate.
OTHER ISSUES CONSIDERED AND DECIDED
The Arbitrators acknowledge that they have each read the pleadings and other materials filed by
the parties.
AWARD
After considering the pleadings, the testimony and evidence presented at the hearing, and any
post-hearing submissions, the Panel has decided in full and final resolution of the issues
submitted for determination as follows:
1. Claimant’s claims are denied in their entirety. Claimant failed to establish that the brokerage
account at issue was subject to the provisions of the Electronic Funds Transfer Act, 15
U.S.C. §1693.
2. Any and all claims for relief not specifically addressed herein, including any requests for
back wages, punitive damages, treble damages, and attorneys’ fees, are denied.
FEES
Pursuant to the Code of Arbitration Procedure, the following fees are assessed:
Filing Fees
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services assessed a filing fee* for each claim:
Initial Claim Filing Fee =$ 1,790.00
*The filing fee is made up of a non-refundable and a refundable portion.
Member Fees
Member fees are assessed to each member firm that is a party in these proceedings or to the
member firm(s) that employed the associated person(s) at the time of the event(s) giving rise to
the dispute. Accordingly, as a party, Respondent is assessed the following:
Member Surcharge =$ 2,640.00
Member Process Fee =$ 5,040.00

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-00179
Award Page 3 of 4
Postponement Fees
Postponements granted during these proceedings for which fees were assessed or waived:
December 17-19, 2025, postponement requested by Claimant =$ 1,690.00
Total Postponement Fees =$ 1,690.00
The Panel has assessed the total postponement fees to Claimant.
Hearing Session Fees and Assessments
The Panel has assessed hearing session fees for each session conducted. A session is any
meeting between the parties and the Arbitrator(s), including a pre-hearing conference with the
Arbitrator(s), which lasts four (4) hours or less. Fees associated with these proceedings are:
Two (2) pre-hearing sessions with the Panel @ $1,690.00/session =$ 3,380.00
Pre-Hearing Conferences: July 15, 2025 1 session
December 12, 2025 1 session
Four (4) hearing sessions @ $1,690.00/session =$ 6,760.00
Hearings: March 11, 2026 2 sessions
March 12, 2026 2 sessions
Total Hearing Session Fees =$ 10,140.00
The Panel has assessed $1,690.00 of the hearing session fees to Claimant.
The Panel has assessed $8,450.00 of the hearing session fees to Respondent.
All balances are payable to FINRA Dispute Resolution Services and are due upon receipt.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-00179
Award Page 4 of 4
ARBITRATION PANEL
Lewis Gordon Brewer - Public Arbitrator, Presiding Chairperson
David J. Millstone - Public Arbitrator
Donald P. Wood - Public Arbitrator
I, the undersigned Arbitrator, do hereby affirm that I am the individual described herein and who
executed this instrument, which is my award.
